title.rendered: Rustic Barn Wedding at Rolling Meadow Farm | Sade + Luke

The rustic barn backdrop was already a dream, but the best part was getting to reconnect with an old friend on his happiest day ever and finally meet his amazing wife. Southern charm, personal flair, and so them from start to finish.

That already makes it personal, but then you add a golden hour that hit just right, a little haze of farm dust floating in the light, and even their new puppy and lots of puppy kisses, and suddenly the whole place felt alive. title.rendered: Surprise Proposal Sarasota | A Romantic Moment to Remember

content.rendered:
Surprise Proposal Sarasota: A Romantic Moment to Remember

Planning a surprise proposal is no small feat, but when it takes place in one of Sarasota’s most beautiful locations, it becomes an unforgettable memory. Sarasota’s Shelby Gardens, with its lush landscapes and serene beauty, offers the perfect backdrop for such a romantic gesture. Let’s dive into Isander’s surprise proposal to Natalie and explore why Sarasota’s Shelby Gardens is the ultimate spot for popping the question.

A BEAUTIFUL Introduction: Shelby Gardens in Sarasota

Stepping into Shelby Gardens feels like entering a botanical paradise. Nestled in the heart of Sarasota, this lush garden is renowned for its stunning greenery, exotic plants, and peaceful atmosphere. It’s a place where love naturally blooms, making it an ideal setting for a surprise proposal.

For Isander, Shelby Gardens wasn’t just a location; it was the perfect canvas to paint a lifelong memory. The vibrant colors of blooming flowers, the gentle rustling of leaves, and the tranquility of the gardens created a scene straight out of a fairytale. It’s no wonder he chose this beautiful spot to ask the love of his life, Natalie, to marry him.

The Big Surprise: A Moment of Pure Joy

Natalie had no idea what was in store when her mom and best friend invited her for a casual outing to Shelby Gardens. Little did she know that Isander, who she thought was on his way to New York, was waiting with a diamond ring and a heart full of love.

The sun shone brightly that day, casting a golden glow over the gardens. With hardly any visitors around, the setting felt intimate and secluded—the perfect atmosphere for a surprise proposal. As Natalie strolled through the gardens, the anticipation was building. Meanwhile, Isander, hidden nearby, was preparing for the big moment.

When Natalie turned a corner, she saw him standing there, a mix of nervousness and excitement on his face. The look in her eyes said it all: pure shock, joy, and love. In that special moment, Isander dropped to one knee and asked the question that would change both their lives forever. Tears streamed down Natalie’s face as she said “Yes!”—a moment neither of them would ever forget.

Why Shelby Gardens is Perfect for Proposals

There’s something truly special about Shelby Gardens that makes it an ideal spot for proposals. Here are just a few reasons:

1. Natural Beauty

Shelby Gardens boasts a variety of picturesque settings, from its stunning banyan trees to its vibrant flower beds. The natural beauty of the gardens provides a romantic and timeless backdrop for any proposal.

2. Peaceful Atmosphere

The serene environment of Shelby Gardens makes it easy to focus on the moment. With quiet paths and secluded spots, couples can enjoy privacy while savoring their special day.

3. Memorable Photo Opportunities

A proposal is a once-in-a-lifetime moment, and Shelby Gardens offers countless beautiful spots for capturing that moment. From the waterfront views to the charming walkways, every corner of the garden is picture-perfect.

4. Convenient Location

Located in Sarasota, Shelby Gardens is easily accessible for locals and visitors alike. Its central location makes it a convenient choice for anyone planning a special event.

Planning Your Own Surprise Proposal in Sarasota

If Isander’s story has inspired you to plan your own surprise proposal, here are some tips to ensure it’s just as meaningful:

1. Choose the Perfect Spot

Shelby Gardens offers several scenic locations, so take some time to explore and pick the one that resonates with your story. Whether it’s a secluded bench or a vibrant flower garden, the setting should reflect your love.

2. Enlist Help

Isander’s proposal was a team effort, with Natalie’s mom and friend helping to make the surprise happen. Don’t be afraid to enlist the help of loved ones or even professional proposal planners.

3. Capture the Moment

Hiring a photographer to discreetly capture the proposal ensures you’ll have beautiful memories to look back on. Shelby Gardens’ natural light and stunning scenery make it easy to get breathtaking shots.

4. Personalize the Experience

Make the proposal uniquely yours by incorporating personal touches. Whether it’s a favorite song, a handwritten note, or a special keepsake, these details make the moment even more meaningful.

5. Consider Timing

Visit Shelby Gardens during a quieter time of day to ensure privacy. Early mornings or weekdays are often less crowded, providing a more intimate experience.

Love Stories Blossom in Sarasota

Sarasota is more than just a beautiful city—it’s a place where love thrives. From the serene beaches to the charming downtown streets, every corner of Sarasota offers a romantic vibe. Shelby Gardens, in particular, stands out as a place where countless couples have celebrated their love stories.

For Isander and Natalie, the gardens will forever hold a special place in their hearts. Their proposal is a testament to the charm of Sarasota and the power of love.

Making Memories That Last a Lifetime

A proposal is one of life’s most memorable moments, and choosing the right location can make all the difference. Shelby Gardens in Sarasota offers the perfect blend of natural beauty, romance, and intimacy, creating a setting that’s as unforgettable as the love story it celebrates.

Whether you’re planning your own surprise proposal or simply looking for inspiration, remember that the most important element is the love you share. Just like Isander and Natalie, your story is unique, and your proposal should reflect the bond that makes your relationship special.

So, take a page from their book and let the charm of Sarasota set the stage for your own unforgettable moment. Shelby Gardens awaits—ready to be part of your love story. content.rendered:
Cold Spring NY Wedding dreams come to life in the scenic Hudson Valley, where nature and charm combine to create unforgettable celebrations. Nestled in the heart of this picturesque region, Cold Spring, NY, served as the perfect backdrop for Zeynep and Dominic’s stunning wedding. With its charming streets, breathtaking river views, and vibrant green foliage, this Hudson Valley treasure set the stage for a remarkable day filled with love and elegance. From the historic West Point Foundry to the Highlands Country Club, every detail was infused with personality and beauty.

A Hudson Valley Gem: Cold Spring, NY

Cold Spring, NY, is a quaint village in the Hudson Valley that exudes charm and serenity. Its rich history, breathtaking landscapes, and unique venues make it an ideal choice for couples seeking a wedding destination that stands out. Zeynep and Dominic’s wedding perfectly captured the essence of what makes a Cold Spring NY Wedding so special.

The couple’s ceremony took place at the historic West Point Foundry. This iconic venue, steeped in history, provided a warm and inviting atmosphere. With its rustic character and scenic surroundings, it created the perfect backdrop for their heartfelt vows. The bride’s best friend officiated, adding a deeply personal touch to their union.

A Reception to Remember

After the ceremony, the celebration continued at The Highlands Country Club. This venue is renowned for its elegance and charm, offering a stunning setting for receptions. However, the true highlight of the evening was the extraordinary dining experience curated by the talented team at Hudson Table.

The vegan menu, crafted with artistry and care, left guests in awe. From vibrant appetizers to decadent main courses, every dish was a testament to the skill and creativity of the chefs. The food wasn’t just a meal; it was an experience, perfectly complementing the love and joy that filled the room.

Unique Details That Stood Out

Zeynep and Dominic’s wedding was anything but traditional. From the moment the bride and groom shared their first look, it was clear this day would be unique. Guests were delighted by the special touches woven into the celebration, including flower tattoos as wedding favors. These simple yet playful gifts added a whimsical element that left everyone smiling.

The couple’s passion for creativity and nature was evident in every aspect of their wedding. From the eco-friendly decor to the personal stories shared during the speeches, their love for the earth and each other shone brightly. It was a joy to witness and capture the connection between Zeynep, a talented Turkish architect, and Dominic, a French environmentalist.

(Continue reading below to discover the breathtaking photos, unique details, and heartfelt moments that made this Cold Spring NY wedding truly unforgettable.)

Nestled in the scenic Hudson Valley, Cold Spring, NY, served as the perfect backdrop for Zeynep and Dominic’s unforgettable Hudson Valley wedding. With its charming streets, stunning river views and the beautiful green foliage, this quaint location provided a magical atmosphere. The wedding ceremony took place at the historic West Point Foundry, a family house filled with lots of character.

Following their heartfelt Hudson Valley wedding ceremony, where the bride’s own best friend helped them say their I do’s, we headed over to their reception at The Highlands Country Club. The true stars here were the incredible chef and staff behind Hudson Table which managed to make the most incredible vegan, drool worthy, delicious, work of art.

This beautiful, elegant and non traditional wedding was incredibly amazing. From the first look, to the special guest walking down the aisle to the incredible meal and drinks. Who knew flower tattoos could get everyone so excited? What a perfect little wedding favor for this Hudson Valley wedding.

It was a true pleasure capturing the love between our Turkish architect bride and environmentalist, earth loving, French groom. We hope this sneak peak shows a bit about their love story, their passion for creativity and nature and all the things and people they love.

Cold Spring, NY is for sure a destination worth exploring. For couples looking for a unique, picturesque location for their Cold Spring NY Wedding, this place is a hidden gem here in New York. With its charming small-town feel, stunning natural scenery and historic venues, it offers everything you need for a dream wedding. Beyond the wedding day, guests can enjoy exploring the many hiking trails, antique shops, coffee shops and scenic spots that Cold Spring has to offer.

Why Choose Cold Spring, NY for Your Wedding?

Cold Spring NY Wedding isn’t just a destination—it’s an experience. Here’s why this Hudson Valley gem is a fantastic choice for couples looking to tie the knot:

1. Breathtaking Scenery

The Hudson River, rolling hills, and lush forests create a stunning natural backdrop. Whether you’re hosting a ceremony by the water or a reception in a historic venue, a Cold Spring NY wedding offers unparalleled beauty.

2. Historic Venues

From the West Point Foundry to elegant country clubs, Cold Spring boasts venues with rich history and unique character. These spaces add a timeless quality to weddings and provide incredible photo opportunities.

3. Small-Town Charm

Cold Spring’s charming streets and welcoming community make it a delightful destination for couples and their guests. The village’s relaxed pace allows everyone to truly savor the celebration.

4. Endless Activities for Guests

Beyond the wedding day, Cold Spring offers plenty of attractions to explore. Guests can enjoy hiking trails with panoramic views, browsing antique shops, sipping coffee in cozy cafes, and taking in the area’s natural beauty.

Tips for Planning a Cold Spring NY Wedding

Cold Spring’s popularity as a wedding destination means venues and accommodations can fill up quickly, especially during peak seasons. Be sure to secure your date well in advance.

2. Embrace the Local Charm

Incorporate elements of a Cold Spring NY Wedding charm into your special day. Consider local flowers for your arrangements, or feature Hudson Valley-inspired dishes on your menu.

3. Explore Outdoor Options

Take advantage of Cold Spring’s stunning landscapes by incorporating outdoor elements into your celebration. Whether it’s a garden ceremony or an outdoor cocktail hour, the natural beauty will elevate your event.

4. Plan for Guest Experiences

Encourage your guests to explore the area by providing a list of local attractions. From scenic hikes to quaint shops, there’s something for everyone in Cold Spring.

5. Work with Local Vendors

Partnering with local vendors ensures a seamless experience and adds a personal touch to your wedding. From florists to photographers, Cold Spring is home to talented professionals who can bring your vision to life.

Celebrating Love in the Heart of the Hudson Valley

Zeynep and Dominic’s wedding was a true celebration of love, creativity, and nature. Cold Spring, NY, provided the perfect setting for their story to unfold, offering a unique blend of elegance and charm. From the historic ceremony venue to the exquisite vegan feast, every detail reflected their personalities and values.

For couples dreaming of a wedding that feels both intimate and extraordinary, a Cold Spring NY Wedding is a destination worth considering. Its scenic beauty, rich history, and welcoming community create an unforgettable experience for couples and their guests alike.

Start Planning Your Cold Spring Wedding

If you’re looking for a wedding destination that combines natural beauty, historic charm, and a touch of magic, Cold Spring NY Wedding makes it the perfect choice. Let this Hudson Valley treasure set the stage for your love story, just as it did for Zeynep and Dominic. With its stunning venues, talented vendors, and endless opportunities for adventure, Cold Spring promises a wedding day you’ll cherish forever.

title.rendered: Family Portrait Photos at Villa del Balbianello | Lake Como

content.rendered:
There are some places in the world that are so beautiful and special that you have to see them with your own eyes to believe them. Villa del Balbianello is one of those places. It is a stunning villa on a small peninsula on Lake Como, with a panoramic view of the water and the mountains. It is a place where history, art, and nature come together in perfect harmony. It is a place where dreams come true.

We had the privilege of visiting this place with our dear friends and lovely Ortega family, who wanted to do a photo shoot in Italy. We’ve been friends since decades ago and it was so much fun to not only have photographed them, but travel with them later on in Switzerland (post coming soon!). When they told us about their idea, we were over the moon. We love Italy, and we love photography. And we love the idea of photographing a beautiful family at Villa del Balbianello.

We started planning months ahead, and when the day arrived, we took a private boat to the villa, feeling like movie stars. And we were not the only ones. The villa has been used as a filming location for several blockbuster movies, such as Star Wars: Episode II – Attack of the Clones and Casino Royale. You can see why when you get there. The villa is so elegant and charming, with its 18th century architecture and its exquisite garden. The garden is full of exotic plants, statues, and topiaries, creating a romantic and whimsical atmosphere. The highlight of the garden is the Loggia Durini, a graceful pavilion that offers the best view of the lake and the mountains.

We had so much fun taking photos at the villa, trying to capture its beauty and our joy. We explored every corner of the garden, finding different angles and backgrounds for our shots. It was not easy to take photos at the villa, though. The villa was very crowded, as it is a popular tourist attraction. We had to be patient and creative, waiting for the right moment to snap our pictures without too many people in them, and of course work our photoshop! But it was 100%worth it. The photos turned out amazing, and we are so happy with them. Check out the end of the post, where you’ll see the Star Wars scene and more info about it 🙂

The last photo on this set is one of our favorites. It is where the Star Wars scene happened, where Anakin Skywalker and Padme Amidala got married in secret. We wanted to recreate that scene with our friends, as a tribute to one of our favorite movies. We asked them to stand on the edge of the loggia, holding hands and looking into each other’s eyes. Then we took the shot.

It was perfect.

We are so grateful for this experience, and for our friends who shared it with us. We feel like we took a little piece of this villa with us, with all of the memories that we captured. We will never forget this place, and how it made us feel.

We hope you enjoyed reading about our family portrait photos at Villa del Balbianello. We hope you get inspired to visit this place someday, and see for yourself how amazing it is. You will not regret it.

Thank you for reading our blog post. Please leave us a comment below and let us know what you think. And if you liked this post, please share it with your friends and family.

title.rendered: Family Photo Shoot at Villa del Balbianello | Lake Como Italy

content.rendered:
Have you ever dreamed of visiting a fairy-tale villa on the shores of a beautiful lake? That’s exactly what we did when we went to Villa del Balbianello, one of the most marvelous places on Lake Como. We were fortunate enough to have our dear friends and gorgeous Artiles family join us for this unforgettable experience. And of course, we couldn’t miss the opportunity to capture some stunning photos of our happy moments together.

Villa del Balbianello is not just a regular villa. It is a movie star. Literally. This magnificent mansion has been featured in some of the most iconic films of all time, such as Star Wars: Episode II – Attack of the Clones and Casino Royale. You might remember the scenes where Anakin and Padme got married, or where James Bond recovered from his injuries. Well, those scenes were filmed right here, at this very spot.

As soon as we arrived at the villa, we felt like we were transported to another world. A world of fantasy, romance, and adventure. A world where anything could happen. We couldn’t believe our eyes when we saw the breathtaking views of the lake and the mountains, the elegant architecture of the villa, and the lush garden that surrounded it. We felt like we were part of a movie ourselves.

And that’s exactly how we wanted to capture our photos. We wanted to make them look like movie posters, with dramatic angles, vibrant colors, and expressive poses. We wanted to show their personality and emotions, as well as our admiration for this place. We wanted to tell a story with our images, a story of friendship, fun, and love.

We had so much fun exploring the villa and finding the best spots for our photos. We took photos from the lake, from the garden, from the loggia, and from inside the villa. We tried to recreate some of the scenes from the movies, as well as invent some of our own. We made memories that will last forever.

We hope you enjoyed reading about the Artiles family photo shoot at Villa del Balbianello. We had an amazing time there, and we are so thankful for our friends who shared this experience with us. If you ever have a chance to visit Lake Como, don’t miss this incredible place. You will feel like a movie star yourself.

Thank you for reading our blog post. Please leave us a comment below and let us know what you think. And if you liked this post, please share it with your friends and family.


































If you are planning to do a photo shoot at Villa del Balbianello, here are some tips that might help you:

    • Book your visit in advance: The villa is open to the public from March to November, but it can get very busy during peak season. You need to book your tickets online or by phone at least a day before your visit. You can also book a guided tour if you want to learn more about the history and the art of the villa.
    • Choose the best time: The best time to take photos at the villa is either early in the morning or late in the afternoon, when the light is softer and more flattering. Avoid midday, when the sun is too bright and harsh. Also, try to avoid weekends and holidays, when the villa is more crowded and noisy.
    • Take a boat: The villa is accessible by land or by water, but we recommend taking a boat if you want to enjoy the full beauty of the lake and the villa. You can rent a boat from one of the nearby towns, such as Lenno or Tremezzo, or hire a private driver who can take you there and wait for you while you visit. The boat ride will also give you some amazing photo opportunities along the way.
    • Explore different angles: The villa offers many different perspectives and backgrounds for your photos. You can take photos from the lake, from the garden, from the loggia, or from inside the villa. Try to capture both wide shots and close-ups, both portraits and landscapes, both candid and posed moments. Experiment with different angles and compositions, and have fun with your creativity.
    • Enjoy yourself: The most important tip for taking great photos at Villa del Balbianello is to enjoy yourself and have fun with your family and friends. Don’t worry too much about getting the perfect shot or following all the rules. Just relax and let your emotions and expressions shine through. The best photos are those that reflect your personality and your happiness.

We hope you enjoyed reading about our family photo shoot at Villa del Balbianello. We had a wonderful time there, and we are so grateful for our friends who shared this adventure with us. If you ever have a chance to visit Lake Como, don’t miss this magical place. You will fall in love with it, just like we did.

title.rendered: 20th Anniversary Photo Session | Mares Family

content.rendered:
It’s not every day that we get to celebrate 20 years of love.  We had the honor of capturing some beautiful moments for our dear friends, the Mares family, who were celebrating their 20th anniversary. 

We chose Matheson Hammock Park in Miami as the location for their photo session, and it was a perfect choice. The park offers a variety of scenic backgrounds, from lush greenery to serene water views. The golden hour light was magical, and the breeze was refreshing.  Claire took this special occasion to use her wedding dress, and she looked truly stunning!  We hope that you enjoy this beautiful 20th anniversary photo session as much as we did.  

And P.S. If you’re planning on taking photos at the Matheson Hammock Park, we’ve left some tips for you at the footer of this blog.

Here are some tips for taking photos at Matheson Hammock Park:

  • Plan ahead and check the weather forecast. The park can get crowded on weekends and holidays, so try to avoid peak hours. Also, be prepared for mosquitoes and bring some repellent.
  • Explore different spots and angles. The park has many hidden gems, such as a coral rock gazebo, a historic coral stone building, and a winding trail. Don’t be afraid to get creative and try different perspectives.
  • If you’re a photographer, use natural light and reflectors. The park has plenty of natural light, especially during sunrise and sunset. You can use reflectors to bounce the light and fill in the shadows. Avoid using flash, as it can create harsh shadows and ruin the mood.
  • Have fun and be yourself. The most important thing is to enjoy the moment and let your personality shine. Don’t worry too much about posing or smiling. Just relax and have fun with your loved ones.

I hope you enjoyed this blog post and got some inspiration for your next anniversary photo session or special occasion. If you are looking for a professional photographer in Miami, feel free to contact us. We would love to work with you and create some amazing memories.

title.rendered: Tree Tops Park Family Photo Shoot

content.rendered:
As passionate photographers, there’s nothing quite like capturing the essence of a beautiful family through portrait photography. Recently, we had the pleasure of working with our friends, with two adorable kids, at the stunning Tree Tops Park in South Florida. In this blog post, I’ll share my experience working with them and offer some tips on how to prepare for a family photo shoot and get the most out of your photographer.

As your friends, we want to make sure that you have the best possible experience and that your family portraits are extra special. That’s why I want to offer some personalized tips for you and any other families who are looking to take some special photos.  Be sure to scroll down to the end of this post for those tips!  We hope that you may enjoy these beautiful photos as much as we did capturing and post-editing them!


























































Tips for a photo shoot at tree tops park

Preparation is key

Firstly, let’s talk about preparation. As a busy family, I know it can be tough to schedule in a photo shoot, but I’m so glad that we were able to find a time that worked for all of us. 

When planning for a family photo shoot, I recommend finding a time when everyone is well-rested and fed, and when the lighting conditions are ideal.  

The golden hour offers a great opportunity to do just that! Choosing a location that is not only visually stunning, but also has some personal significance is another great way to make your photos extra special.

What to wear for a photo shoot?

When it comes to preparing for the shoot, I always suggest coordinating outfits that complement each other.   It’s also important to choose clothing that is comfortable and practical, especially for little ones who like to run around and explore. 

If you have any special props or requests, don’t hesitate to let us know, so we can work together to create a personalized shoot that reflects your family’s unique style.

How to make the best out of your photo session

During the shoot, our goal is to make everyone feel at ease and comfortable in front of the camera, so we can capture those natural, candid moments that truly showcase your family’s personality. I always recommend getting the kids involved and making the shoot fun and interactive, so we can create some genuine moments of joy and laughter.  

And of course, offering a nice treat to the movies and popcorn or an ice cream goes a long way to get them motivated 🙂

 In terms of technical tips for pro photographers, I always suggest shooting with a fast lens and using a wide aperture to achieve a beautiful bokeh effect and to make your subjects pop. It’s also important to pay attention to your shutter speed and ISO settings to ensure that your photos are sharp and well-exposed.  Lightning is everything!

As your friends and photographers, our goal is to make your family portrait photo shoot extra special and to create images that you will treasure for years to come. I hope these personalized tips will help make your experience even better, and I can’t wait to share the final results with you soon!

Much love,

Ale, Italo & the Acromatico Family

title.rendered: Ritz Carlton Wedding | Coconut Grove | Pakistani & Syrian | Kawthr + Hamad

content.rendered:
What happens when you blend two amazing cultures?  The story of two beautiful souls that become one, and priceless memories to cherish for a lifetime.  Kawthr and Hamad chose us to be their photographers for their multicultural Ritz Carlton Wedding. With a gorgeous Syrian bride and a handsome Pakistani groom, this wedding was the perfect blend of both worlds!

It was an absolutely beautiful day for a wedding! The Ritz Carlton in Coconut Grove was the perfect setting for this Pakistani/Syrian Arabic couple’s big day. We were so excited to be the ones capturing all of the memories of this event!

The bride and groom were absolutely glowing with happiness, and their families were so supportive and loving. The whole day was filled with so much joy, laughter, and love. Their wedding day couldn’t have been more beautiful and amazing without the Jannette Alvarez Designs team for providing all of the gorgeous elements to this wedding. From the amazing floral arrangements to the beautiful outdoor ceremony setup. This all couldn’t have happened without the gorgeous Ritz Carlton Coconut Grove staff and beautiful venue that was just perfect for their big day. From colors to sparklers, fog machines to outfit reveals, this wedding day was a true show stopper. This Pakistani & Syrian wedding closed off our last wedding of 2022 with a bang!

Thank you Kawthr and Hamad for trusting us to capture one of the most important days of your lives. Ladies and gents, this wasn’t all.  Enjoy Day #1 of the Pakistani wedding.  Stay tuned for part two of their Syrian Arabic Wedding celebrations and be sure to check out some tips at the bottom of this blog if you’re planning a wedding at the Ritz Carlton in Coconut Grove.































































































If you’re planning a wedding at the Ritz Carlton in Coconut grove, here are a few tips for you:

Tip #1. When choosing a relaxed luxury wedding venue, The Ritz Carlton in Coconut Grove, Miami is an exquisite venue for a Pakistani wedding.

We’ve always loved the Ritz Carlton brand.  They stand out for experiences, will make you feel like a king and a queen, and leave you wanting more.  The Ritz Carlton in Coconut Grove, Miami is truly a beautiful venue for a Pakistani wedding! The property is located on beautiful Biscayne Bay, and features an outdoor terrace that is perfect for an outdoor ceremony or reception. The Ritz Carlton also has a variety of event spaces that can accommodate a large wedding party. And, of course, the staff at the Ritz Carlton is renowned for their impeccable service. If you are looking for a luxurious setting for your Pakistani wedding, the Ritz Carlton in Coconut Grove, Miami is a perfect choice.

Tip #2. Plan in advance to make sure your RITZ CARLTON wedding IS WHAT YOU’VE ALWAYS DREAMED OF.

The day you get married is one of the most important days of your life. You want everything to be perfect, and that includes your wedding. If you’re planning a Pakistani wedding, here are some tips to make sure everything goes off without a hitch.

1. Plan ahead. Pakistani weddings are usually very large and can take months to plan. Make sure you start planning early so you have enough time to get everything done.

2. Choose your venue carefully. The venue is one of the most important aspects of your wedding. Make sure you choose a venue that is large enough to accommodate all of your guests and that is centrally located so everyone can easily get to it.

3. Hire an experienced professional wedding photographer. Your wedding only happens once.  Your special day will be very fast paced.  Your wedding photos will be cherished memories, so you want to make sure they’re perfect. Hire a photographer who you trust and is experienced in shooting weddings so that you’re guaranteed beautiful photos.

4. Get all your vendors on the same page. From the caterer to the entertainment, you need to make sure all your vendors are on the same page and perfectly matched with the theme of your wedding.

5. Create a timeline. Have a rehearsal, ceremony and reception timeline so everyone knows what needs to happen and when. This will help you stay organized and stress-free leading up to the big day.

6. Have a backup plan. Anything can happen, so have some backup plans in place so that if something goes wrong you’ll have a plan B.  This is where hiring a professional wedding planner can help alleviate a lot of your day’s challenges.  

7. Take care of yourself. Leading up to the wedding, make sure you’re taking time for yourself to relax, exercise and get plenty of rest so that you look and feel your best on the big day.

3. Make sure to take advantage of the Ritz CARLTON’S fabulous WEDDING catering options!

The Ritz Carlton culinary team will work with you one-on-one to make sure that your Wedding Day meal is perfect. They offer an array of cuisine options to choose from, ranging from Italian, Asian, and Mediterranean dishes, to seafood and American favorites. 

The really cool thing about them is that you can create a customized menu for the wedding reception. Their meeting & special events manager will be glad to custom design your menu and special order any food or beverage item not currently available on their menus.

4. Have a blast at your wedding!

If you are planning a wedding at the Ritz Carlton in Coconut Grove, you will be able to take advantage of the beautiful setting and the wonderful amenities that the hotel has to offer. The hotel staff will be able to help you with all of the details of your wedding, and you will be able to enjoy the day with your family and friends. 

Be sure to take advantage of your wedding photographer to take some beautiful photos that you can cherish for a lifetime.  With the Ritz Carlton’s expertise and an experienced wedding photographer that can capture your candid story as your wedding day happened, your special day will be one to remember.

title.rendered: Vizcaya Portrait Session

content.rendered:
Vizcaya has always been a playground for us as photographers, because it has so much to offer. From the beautiful mansion that’s an iconic place to visit in South Florida, to the gorgeous manicured gardens that are always breathtaking to see.

When we bring our clients here, we love seeing their reactions. When they see the new scenes for their photo shoot, even though Vizcaya is one place for their photoshoot, it truly offers so many different scenarios. The bay is definitely one of our favorites, along with the European vibes that you can’t get anywhere else but in Vizcaya Museums and Gardens (or Italy, if you want to take a 10-hour flight with us!).

Yesenia & Jason have been married for 20 years, and we’re thrilled to have been able to celebrate part of their 20th anniversary in this special setting. They shared with us that their wedding photos were not what they were expecting, so they were hoping to keep memories for their children that they’ll be proud to show off. We hope that we did that for you guys! It was truly an honor serving you, and seeing these amazing and incredible memories come to life. Enjoy some of our favorites from this Vizcaya portrait session (and more tips below on how you can create amazing memories in Vizcaya towards the end of the photos!).

How to make the best out of your photo shoot at Vizcaya Museum and Gardens

Arrive early. There’s so much to do and see at Vizcaya that with 2 hours you would not be able to cover everything. Plus, between obtaining and paying for your photo permits, to walking inside in the dozens of acres, you’re going to want that extra time. We always recommend around 2.5 to 3 hours of coverage to be able to maximize your day at Vizcaya.

Hire a professional photographer that knows Vizcaya well.

This is very important because you wouldn’t want to miss incredible details of the place and Vizcaya’s remarkable surroundings on your photos. From the iconic (and little hidden) coral cave to the famous ironman movie scenes, you’re going to feel as if you’re taking photos in Europe while being in Miami.

Did you see the scene from Ironman? (3:25) This beautiful European house is now even more famous than before because of its appearance on this superhero Marvel movie. And quite frankly, if they chose it as a location, it should tell you that Vizcaya is quite remarkable.

How to dress for your Vizcaya photo session

In our honest opinion, we feel that this place is grand, so anything goes. Don’t be afraid to show up on a gala dress or a delicate, light & airy look. Nothing is too much. We do recommend 2 outfits if you’re looking to have a variety of scenes taken, and if you’re wondering where to change, they have a place on site.

How to hire the best professional photographer for your Vizcaya photo shoot

When it comes to a special occasion, such as your proposal, engagement, anniversary, portrait, quinceañera, or even your wedding, you will want to choose a photographer with an incredible reputation that guarantees your images will be what you want, and more. You’re making an investment, and your experience and outcome are truly important.

Time after time, we come across couples that regret their decisions so much on choosing a photographer on a whim, because they didn’t think of the impact that this was going to cause on their lives. Knowing that these will be the images you will choose for your children and the children of your children for generations to come. Don’t take it light, because these moments will never return. And since you’re already making an important investment, make it on the right photographer.

Ask all of the questions that you would want answered fearlessly, and have a one-on-one sit down before making a commitment, to know if you are on the same page. A photographer is not just there to take photos. They’re there to create fine art, and we truly believe that every image created should be its own masterpiece.

If you’re looking for a professional photographer that can rock your socks, feel free to reach out to us. Let’s connect and see if we’re the right fit for each other 🙂

title.rendered: Family Beach Sunrise Photos | Cardiello Family

content.rendered:
Time for our yearly family sessions to start rolling. We LOVE it when its this family’s turn as they are dear friends and truly so special to us

This year they decided to go for a different look so we headed at 6AM to Pompano beach for a sunrise photo session. Worth is? 100%!! The only way to get the water and crazy sky colors is at sunrise for South Florida and trust me, those colors will not disappoint, they are simply incredible!

Enjoy some of our favorites from this beautiful family! P.S. Make sure to scroll down all the way to the bottom, you’ll find great tips to create your perfect family beach sunrise photos!

How to Get the Perfect Family Beach Sunrise Photos

Have you ever looked at a photo of a beach sunrise and thought, “I want to take a picture like that!”?  With a little planning and a lot of patience, you can get the perfect family beach sunrise photos.

Here are a few tips to make it happen:

1,  Choose your location. You’ll want to find a spot with a good view of the sun as it rises. 

2.  Wake up early! This is the most important step – you’ll want to be at your chosen location at least 30 minutes before sunrise. 

3.  Setup your camera, tripod or light.

4.  Wait for the sun to rise. 

5.  Finally, don’t forget to enjoy the moment!

If you’re looking to hire a professional photographer, by following these simple tips, you’ll be sure to get a stunning photo of the perfect family beach sunrise.

It all starts with the perfect location

 When it comes to getting the perfect family beach sunrise photo, the location is key. You’ll want to find a spot with a clear view of the horizon so you can clearly see the sun as it rises. If there are trees or buildings in the way, it may block the sun and ruin your shot. Consider the time of year when you’ll be taking your photo. If you’re planning to take your photo, you’ll want to make sure the sun will be in the right place. Research the exact sunrise time In the city that you’re planning on shooting, on the date that you’re looking to take your family beach sunrise photos.

The early bird gets the shot

Waking up early is the most important step in getting the perfect family beach sunrise photo. You’ll want to be at your chosen location at least 30 minutes before sunrise. This will give you time to set up your location, and your photographer will get setup with their camera and take a few test shots. The good thing about hiring a professional photographer Is that they’ll be ready for that sunrise hour, with their lights and right lenses, to produce an artistic shot of your family. 

Framing is everything

Once you have your camera set up, it’s time to start taking photos. But before you start snapping away, take a moment to think about framing. If you want to include the whole family in the photo, you’ll need to make sure everyone fits in the frame. You’ll also want to pay attention to the background. A busy background can distract from the main subject of the photo, which should be the sunrise. So try to find a spot with a plain background, like the sky or the ocean. 

Capturing the moment

 As the sun starts to rise, you’ll want to start taking photos. But don’t just take any photo—try to capture the feeling of the moment. The best way to do this is to take a series of photos, so you can choose the one that best captures the feeling of the sunrise. To get a sweeping view of the sunrise, you can take a panorama. Or, if you want to focus on the sun itself, you can try taking a close-up. Just remember to keep your camera steady, as even the slightest movement can ruin a close-up photo if you’re not using flash or strobe lights.

Don’t forget the details

Once you have your main photo, it’s time to start thinking about the details. What kind of photos will tell the story of your sunrise experience? You might want to take a photo of the shadows the sun is casting, or the way the light reflects off the water. Or, if you’re taking a photo of your family, you might want to capture a candid moment.

Use natural light

One of the best things about sunrise photos is the natural light. The light at sunrise is unlike any other time of day, so you’ll want to make sure you take advantage of it. To get the most beautiful light, try taking your photos in the first few minutes after the sun rises. This is when the light is at its best. 

And…ACTION!

Now that you know how to take the perfect family beach sunrise photo, it’s time to get out there and start taking pictures! Just remember to have fun and enjoy the moment.  And by all means, we recommend hiring a professional photographer at least once per year, to capture your beautiful growing family year after year.

Think about how beautiful your walls are going to be full of family memories.  Imagine how It’s going to feel 10, 20 years from now, to see your family wall and look back at those priceless memories.

You’re building moments and memories that last for a lifetime.  And there’s no better way of doing It than by capturing the perfect family beach sunrise photos with your professional photographer.

title.rendered: Robbins Preserve Maternity Session | Carol + Diego

content.rendered:
It’s definitely not every day I get to witness my bestie (that also happens to be my sister in law) as a mommy to be and my dear brother as a daddy to be! This Robbins Preserve Maternity Session will forever be a dear one to our hearts. Carol and Diego are expecting their first baby boy coming next February! And we decided to celebrate this special moment by freezing some beautiful memories forever.

We went to Robbins Preserve park in Davie, FL to shoot this maternity session because Carol was set on a natural country session with lots of greens. This park offers exactly that! Full of trees and open spaces it’s the perfect space to do any kind of photoshoot that feels country and relaxed.

Baby Noah, auntie Ale and uncle Italo can’t wait to meet you! Enjoy some of our favorites for this beautiful afternoon:

When is the best time to book your maternity shoot at the Robbins preserve?

So you’re set on location.  Let’s say that going to take your maternity photos at the Robbins Preserve in South FL.  The timing of your baby bump is key, and you want to make sure it is displayed prominently. Most expectant mothers will book their photo shoot to take place at the beginning of the third trimester so there will be no doubt about their belly size. 

Some mothers choose to wait a little longer to give their belly more time to grow, but don’t wait too long! You never know if your little one is going to decide to make an early arrival. 

It can be challenging to move around in the last few weeks of your pregnancy, and you may be very uncomfortable. It’s best if you have your shoot completed while you still have some level of comfort, before it becomes difficult for you to move around.

What to wear for your maternity photo shoot at the robbins preserve?

It’s absolutely up to you!  At the end of the day, it will reflect your personality, your style, your vibe! The objective of a maternity shoot is to showcase that beautiful and historic baby bump. Therefore, it would be counterproductive to wear maternity clothes that cover it and make it difficult to see.  If you’re taking photos at the Robbins Preserve, you’re probably going to want that country / nature vibe.  

The most popular clothing item is a form-fitting maternity dress. Perhaps you may feel uneasy about putting your belly on full display in this way, but we can assure you that your photos will be beautiful. You can still choose a loose-fitting dress — just keep in mind that your photographer will probably have you gather it and hold it close to your belly.

The bonds between an expectant mother and her loved ones should be captured in maternity photos.

 If you have a partner, they should be present in some, and you can include siblings or pets as well.  Here’s an example of a family maternity session that we photographed at the Robbins Lodge recently.  Pregnancy is a time of connection and love, so showing the bond between an expectant mother and her partner should be an important part of a photo shoot. 

Your photographer will usually take several photos of both parents and encourage you to get as close as possible.  If the siblings are young, we recommend starting the maternity photo session with them included, as kids may not be able to pose for a long time. A maternity photo shoot is also a great way for the siblings to feel included in the pregnancy.

What we love about the Robbins Preserve in Davie, is that it is a perfect space for families to come together.  There are lot’s of lush trees, landscapes, and shaded areas in case of a hot summer day. 

Feel beautiful, feel confident.

A maternity photo shoot is an excellent opportunity for you to feel beautiful and confident about your baby bump. You will also be creating precious memories that will last forever.  

It is essential that you select a photographer that makes you feel comfortable and has previously taken pictures that you love. If you are not impressed by a photographer’s portfolio, choose somebody else.

This is your opportunity to show off all the hard work and dedication the last nine months have demanded of you, so don’t be afraid to embrace it.

But now with one extra addition to the family, their beautiful daughter!

This place, even though is small and always crowded, you can always count on getting good photos! Looks so different throughout the year with all the flowers blooming and greenery all around.

Best part is that its free of charge unlike many photography spots in Florida so if you are looking for that European vibe this place can definitely deliver!

Check out some of our favorites from this beautiful family and their anniversary session:

Coral Gables Entrance Park: A Photographic Tour

Coral Gables Entrance Park is one of the most popular tourist destinations in the city. The park is known for its beautiful gardens, diverse wildlife, and stunning views of the cityscape. If you’re looking for a place to take some professional photos, this is the perfect spot!

We’ve been professional photographers for years, and have had the opportunity to photograph many of the city’s most popular attractions. But there’s something about Coral Gables Entrance Park that always leaves us feeling inspired.

Whether you’re a professional photographer or just someone who enjoys taking pictures, we hope you’ll enjoy this photographic tour of the park!

A beautiful place to relax and take in the scenery

As you enter the park, you’ll instantly be surrounded by beautiful gardens and lush vegetation.

These gorgeous Bugambilia flowers, European-style pergola, and bright blue skies make the portraits artistic and come to life.

We also love the water pond and the reflection it makes on the photos, with the serenity vibes it gives.

Take your photos at the Coral Gables Entrance Park!

Whether you’re a professional photographer or just someone who enjoys taking pictures, we hope you’ll enjoy taking photos at the Coral Gables Entrance Park.

The park is truly a unique place, and there’s no shortage of photo opportunities.

If you’re looking for a place to take your family or friends, Coral Gables Entrance Park is the perfect spot. There’s something for everyone to enjoy, and you’re sure to create lasting memories.

title.rendered: Greenpoint Loft Wedding – Brooklyn | Nessa + Torry

content.rendered:
Just a few months ago, we were together with Nessa and Torry for their engagement session. By the time the session ended, we nearly froze to death. Clearly, the cold loves us. Despite spring finally springing, it was a nice and cool day on this Brooklyn wedding day.

Before heading to The Williamsburg Hotel we did a pit stop at Upstate Stock to get fueled up for the day. It’s our fave! Nessa awaited us at her gorgeous suite with the best city views and a beautiful smile. She was as zen as she could be!

If you’re getting married or are undecided about doing a first look, keep scrolling, you might change your mind! We are all in when it comes to first looks. You get to have an intimate moment, get a really awesome groom reaction AND you get bonus time added to your wedding day timeline! How? Once the first look is in, we get to squeeze in all the bridal and couple photos that usually we tend to rush for after the ceremony. What’s the big deal anyway? – you might ask – Well, you get to have MORE couple photos and you don’t risk the chance of loosing the beautiful daylight if things run behind.

Nessa and Torry did just that! We went to The Boathouse at Prospect Park for their first look. This bride left her groom speechless, even shedding a few tears! We’re not crying, you’re crying!

After this, we headed to the Greenpoint Loft where they had their rustic, boho, eclectic ceremony and reception. I do have to say, if you’re looking for amazing New York skyline views, this is the place! Keep scrolling to get a little taste of Nessa and Torry’s wedding. There were even Nigerian traditional outfits for this one!

Thank you guys for trusting me for your wedding day. Here’s a little sneak of this Brooklyn wedding at the Greenpoint Loft:

title.rendered: Robbins Preserve Family Session | Campilii Family

content.rendered:
Where do you even begin when you get to shoot your favorite people at Robbins Park for their family session?

First of all, I’m extremely proud to call you my family: brother, sister and coolest nephews in the world. Ale and Italo, you’ve taught me everything I know and I owe it all to the two of you. You’ve gifted me my career and I’m happy to be able to gift you these memories that I know you’ll be able to remember forever.

There’s so much to say but I’ll stick to talking about the family session. As you might already know, anything you carefully plan doesn’t always turn out the way you want it. Pinterest cakes, the perfect beach day, your wedding, you get the point. Naturally, this session was not planned. We picked out this random Monday afternoon (2 days before), Ale picked out the outfits and we barely knew where we would go to the actual photoshoot location.

I’ve known Ale since I was 13 and she’s always been a wild one. Barefoot, crazy, gorgeous hair, organic queen, natural hippie. Italo is probably the complete opposite, or he likes to pretend he is at least LOL. You can only imagine what great mix Matteo and Lorenzo are. Can’t really explain them but I know these photos will say everything you do need to know and feel about them. My two favorite photographers, couple, little family, all in one gallery. Between Italo’s ugly laughs, Ale running around barefoot, Matteo in his habitat and Lorenzo doing his thing, we had so much fun.

If there’s one thing I’ve definitely learned during covid is take these moments in. Be simple. Stay simple. Enjoy the simple things.

I miss you guys insanely, love you to the moon and back. Thank you for letting me make memories with you and capture them. Hope you enjoy:

title.rendered: VIP Country Club Indian Wedding, NY – Tessa + Kurien

content.rendered:
We met Tessa and Kurien on their very own wedding day. The day couldn’t have been more perfect. End of July with zero humidity and a sunny day? I’ll take it!

How did we meet? This very day. It’s true. We don’t get to meet many of our bride and grooms until their wedding day. We usually prefer to even do an engagement session prior to the big day so we have a chance to bond and form a relationship.

Despite meeting Tessa and Kurien right on the spot, it was as if we had known each other from forever ago. We got introduced thanks to Tessa’s sorority sister, Christine, which happens to be one of our clients that became family along with her hubby Shawn.

THIS is what I love about what we do. We meet clients. Form a bond. And we’re united forever. POOF! Just like that. I mean, how could there not be a union after literally being there to capture thee most important day of your lives with your family and friends. Our clients are just the best. They’re family.

Ok. Also, another fun fact here is that Tessa and I ADORE Maggie Sottero. Yes. We ended up finding out after the wedding that we both got married in Maggie Sottero wedding gowns. This kind of makes us wedding dress sisters? I totally just made up a new thing. But it’s a thing now.

title.rendered: Connecticut Micro Wedding | Tammy + Tay

content.rendered:
For this next post, I got to work once again with Tammy and Tay, this time, for their Connecticut Micro Wedding.

You might remember these cuties from their fabulous Cold Spring engagement sesh in early May. I made them get in the water and get a little wet and they totally rocked with it.

For their wedding, we did something a little similar..except it was kind of not predicted, unwanted and definitely not planned: It RAINED. They got wet. Did it for the gram, jk! They did do it for me and the love of art 🙂

Ok. So this is where we need to get real and have a r e a l talk:

Your wedding will NOT ever go as planned. If it does, good! One thing you will never ever be able to predict is the weather. These are the things you risk also while having an outdoor wedding. As much planning as you can do, you can never plan for this. Will it sprinkle? Will it pour? No one will ever know. What I do know is rain never stops me. I will always make sure to go above and beyond in any creative way to make your wedding photos be YOU and express y o u r wedding day through images.

Emotions live in photos. Moments get captured. Rain doesn’t ever stop that.

Did I get wet? Yes. Was I prepared? Not fully. Why? Because I didn’t know the duration of the rain, how heavy it would pour, the exact location of the wedding, how much space I have to work with. These are all things as photographers we always strive to be creative with. I live for the unknown and intend to shine in the rain. No matter what, I want my couples to still be able to look back at their wedding day (rain or thunder) and remember “them”. Remember how they looked. Most importantly, remember how they felt.

I have to say, we truly need to normalize more weekday micro weddings. They’re absolutely intimate and a true delight. For Tammy and Tay, having most of their closest family and friends to celebrate their union was just perfect.

It rained. It poured. They shined. All smiles.

Thank you Tammy and Tay for letting me capture the most important parts of your micro wedding and for giving me your smiles and warmth on this rainy day.

This wedding I have to say was very special. Not only did we get to work with one of the happiest grooms we’ve ever met but we also felt like we became a part of Tintu and Nirmal’s (aka Nemo’s) family.

We had the pleasure of shooting their engagement session earlier this spring, with the most prettiest cherry blossom season right here in New York. What does this have to do with their wedding day? It actually rained on both days! By rain, I mean, it poured on their wedding day.

There are clients you meet in life that you just know you’ll always remember the memories forever. Clients that become family.

I got to work with the most amazing team ever thanks to Amee and her team at Karma Events. They made sure the wedding process went smoothly (and it did!). Everything was absolutely flawless. It was as if a bunch of friends got together while the rest of us ensured we did our end. A big collab. From the DJ to the sparklers, the baristas to the decor, the photobooth to the music, the MC. The smoke to the amazing energy on the floor. Everything. It all came alive. I got to live it and feel it. All the love you could feel right in one room. All the emotions, the colors, the vibrancy, the LIFE. Life that almost felt extinct. It came back right this night seeing everyone come to life. Dances. Smiles. Music. Nothing really mattered, just the moment. Living this night was everything.

I had the best team to experience this with thanks to my girl Heather and our awesome video crew.

title.rendered: Cold Spring, NY – Engagement | Tammy + Tay

content.rendered:
Photography is the gift that keeps on giving, after all, that’s exactly how we end up meeting most of our clients: through their very own friends.

When I met Tammy, she was walking one of our dear friend’s wedding. I never would’ve thought, just a few months later we’d be here, shooting her very own engagement sesh.

We had been planning the perfect session but the weather just didn’t seem to want to cooperate. New York has so many awesome locations and it was so hard to narrow it down to Cold Spring, but we did! The day was absolutely amazing and it was probably one of our warmest “spring” days. Cold Spring is one of my personal favorite locations, I mean, it’s got the best creamery (shot out to Moo Moo’s) and who doesn’t love Cold Spring Coffeehouse.

I don’t know about you, but this sesh was giving me all the Costa Rica vibes. For a second, I forgot I was in NY. Maybe it’s just that part of me that’s itching to hop on a plane or the fact that the tropical lands are screaming my name. Either way, Cold Spring was magical.

Tammy and Tay were such a pleasure to work with. They were absolutely down for anything, I made them get in the water and also decided to join them while my pale little feet absorbed the cold water and slipped on the rubbery rocks. Needless to say, it was worth it!

We had quite the audience and apparently were the entertainment of this spring afternoon in Cold Spring. Three washed up dead fishies landed on the rocks, and truthfully, they made art.

What do I personally love most about photography? Feeding off of people’s energies. I get what I receive and photograph what I feel. Thank you Tammy and Tay for letting me capture your story. I can’t wait to shoot your wedding! In the meantime, enjoy some of my faves:

title.rendered: Central Park Indian Engagement | Tintu + Nemo

content.rendered:
They met during college almost 10 years ago, it was not love at first sight (for her) and he quickly got friend zoned. Of course, that all changed! This is the story of Tintu and Nemo.

Before we scroll down to the pictures we took in Central Park for this Indian Engagement session, I have to tell you a little bit about this sesh.

Tintu and Nemo contacted us during their quarantine and we scheduled the “perfect” spring day in April. They haven’t had the easiest year, after all, having to move their wedding plans from India to here is quite a challenge, especially during the middle of a pandemic. Ok, so spring time in the city that doesn’t sleep: birds chirping, sweet flowers perfuming the air, dogs walking their owners, talented violinists play for a buck, runners taking over the sidewalks, spring is in the a i r…or so you think!

Ok so exactly just that was happening but as soon as my shutter made its first click.. little raindrops came down…Welp. Do we reschedule, do we stop, do we keep going? Milliseconds to make a decision and the one thing I knew is we were shooting in Central Park rain or shine! So we did!

Played around with mega bubbles (who doesn’t love bubbles??). Hid under the Bethesda Terrace along with everyone else that was stuck in Central Park with the rain. While they stayed dried, I shot! Surprisingly it did stop raining, the people in the park went back to line dancing out in the open, dogs were back on their leashes, children back on their skates and we were back at shooting.

The best part was (at the right perfect time) a tiny bit of sun ray came out of nowhere to give me a natural spotlight. Can’t complain. We had a blast shooting Tintu and Nemo at Central Park for their Indian Engagement Session. Absolutely can’t wait for the wedding next month! title.rendered: Secret Gardens Miami Engagement Session

content.rendered:
Miami is a beautiful, vibrant city with a lot to offer. From the beaches to the nightlife, there is something for everyone in this city. And one of the best things about Miami is this amazing place: The Secret Gardens Miami.

Meet Jane + Denzel: a proposal story.

You know when you meet couples you know they are meant to be with each other? That’s exactly the case of Jane and Denzel. Both beautiful people with a huge heart. We felt honored to be part of this surprise proposal for Jane.

Denzel planned everything months in advance. He wanted his proposal to be picture-perfect, and decided to pop the question at the gorgeous Secret Gardens Miami. This place has to be one of the most EPIC locations in Miami. So beautiful for any occasion and perfect for this memorable one.

Please enjoy some of our favorites from this beautiful couple. Can’t wait till their wedding! And don’t forget to checkout our tips below for taking your photos at the Secret Gardens in Miami.

Secret Gardens Miami: A Guide for your photography portraits

This hidden gem is a photographer’s paradise. It is a great place to escape the hustle and bustle of the city and enjoy some peace and quiet.

In this blog post, we will take you on a tour of the Secret Gardens in Miami. We will show you a gorgeous proposal that we got to witness, and give you some tips on how to take great photos while you are there.

What makes the Secret Gardens in Miami so special?

The 6-acre property, with its lush vegetation, six crystalline waterfalls, and koi-filled ponds that house three breathtaking swans, is a once-in-a-lifetime backdrop.

Throughout the beautiful gardens you can also find various sets, stone arches, and bridges that you can use to enhance your unique shots.

Each turn you make on the property provides a different view, allowing for a vast variety of scenery, making the Secret Gardens Miami one of the top photography location in South Florida.

The best time to take your photos

The best time to take your photos at the Secret Gardens Miami is early in the morning or late in the afternoon. This is when the light is soft and the gardens are less crowded.

We recommend planning to take photos during the week, to avoid less crowds and have more ample space for yourself. And by all means, we highly recommend renting the canoe or any of the props that they offer. It will definitely make your portrait photo session very unique!

One of the things that makes secret gardens so special is that they are a great place to escape the hustle and bustle of the city. Secret Gardens Miami is a photographer’s paradise.

With their lush vegetation, gorgeous fish pond and waterfall, it’s an exotic place that offers plenty of opportunities for great photos.

Photography tips for shooting at the Secret Gardens in Miami

When taking photos in the Secret Gardens in Miami, be sure to take advantage of the lush vegetation. Use plants and flowers as foreground elements in your photos to add depth and interest.

And don’t forget to explore the hidden nooks and crannies of the garden. This is where you will find the most interesting subjects for your photos.

How much is the Secret Gardens Miami rental for photo shoots?

Their photography permit rental rates are $255 and include:

Three hours of access to the entire Secret Gardens Miami property, including the Villa Laguna. You will have a private, air-conditioned dressing room as well as sitting areas for guests.

Additionally, sets are available to use at no additional cost and private bathrooms are also included. You’re welcome bring the photographer of your choice.

Hire a professional photographer for your life’s special events

We’re a humble team of award-winning photographers who are truly passionate about what we do, and we absolutely love capturing memories that last a lifetime.

If you’re looking for a professional photographer to capture your memories: whether it’s for your wedding, engagement, proposal, senior or individual portraits, family portraits, maternity or your special event, look no further.

title.rendered: Pawling, NY – Snow Portrait | Tifah

content.rendered:
What do Sunday’s and snow days have in common? My camera.

Woke up to a text. 2 hours later we met up. Where to? Wherever. Headed to Pawling Park. Nothing was planned. The colors were perfect. Tifah absolutely rocked this by just being herself. A true winter wonderland: playground version.

Free spirited, happy and go with the flow. Loved her energy during this shoot. It was absolutely one I will never forget. Not only did we get to shoot in a fun playground in the middle of a snow fall but we shot right on a frozen lake. Yes. I totally overcame my childhood fear of falling inside a frozen lake. Also, if you know me, I’m not really scared of much except everything and nothing. T e r r i f i e d of bees (but overcame that too). Honestly, these Disney movies get you (thanks Balto!). I knew the only way I’d be on a frozen lake was if it was absolutely worth it. Give me a camera and I’ll probably go sky diving (jk) but you get the point.

Oh. There was also a big fat warning sign that said THIN ICE.

We fell a few times, laughed so hard I fell again. I had as much fun shooting as I did watching this girl just be her. I also think that’s super important. Just be you. It’ll show. Photos don’t lie.

title.rendered: Arlington, MA – Micro Wedding | Elle + Hector

content.rendered:
A New York Wedding. That’s what they initially had planned.

Many of our dear brides have had to really change their wedding plans, pushing dates, having smaller weddings, sacrificing wedding parties, honeymoon and dear ones not being there.

In the case of Elle and Hector, they had to do all that while having to change all their prepaid wedding plans and move their wedding all the way to Massachusetts. When we found out their wedding turned into a destination, micro wedding, we couldn’t be even more excited to be a part of their big day.

They got ready at the Archer Hotel in Burlington, MA. The industrial vibes and the amazing decor was truly a photography playground. To make the most of daylight, I recommended doing a first look. It was sooooo worth it (we’re not crying, you’re crying and so were they!)

This really helped take advantage of some natural light (while we still had it). Sunset at 4:20pm is not fair but we made the best of it and the best use of our light while we could!

Despite this being a micro wedding, over 500 of their family and friends attended live via Zoom. It all turned out to be one big virtual party with a virtual DJ and all. Nothing was going to stop Elle and Hector from partying on their big day.

Also, let’s take a moment to focus on the flowers arrangements. I mean, they were a complete show stopper. Once again, @juniperglenn_ killed it with the flower decor. Pro tip: flowers really make a difference. Don’t you ever skip them! They bring life and color to the room and truly make a huge difference in the whole ambiance. They also brightened up this whole blog post.

title.rendered: Beacon, NY – Engagement | Elle + Hector

content.rendered:
He reserved a bookstore. She loves books. Hid a love story book in the shelves and then hid the ring inside a box that looked like a book. Binnacle Books in Beacon, NY. This is where Hector proposed to Elle (virtually), because you know, covid.

Fast forward to their engagement session, only a few days before their upcoming wedding in Boston. Right in that same Bookstore where she said: YES! Our friends at Binnacle Books were so kind as to let us make a reservation to shoot their engagement session in this special place.

We then walked around Beacon, quickly stopped at Glazed Over Donuts, because you know, you donut say no to donuts!

It was definitely a challenge racing after the sun especially with the time change. Elle and Hector just went with it and were so easy to work with. She said she was a little “camera shy” but I didn’t buy it. She quickly eased into it and they barely even felt I was there. This is such a KEY thing to do before your wedding. Book an engagement session. It makes a HUGE difference for your wedding day. Once you’re warmed up to the camera it’ll be so much easier on your big day.

Fun fact. I hate being in front of the camera. We too took engagement photos (with Acromatico as well! Shot out to Ale and Italo) and it sure made it easier for me to be a bit less camera shy on the day of our wedding.

Another fun fact. It was supposed to rain. I told them to bring a cute umbrella, so they did, because they’re awesome! It was also clear. Double awesome! OF COURSE, it didn’t end up raining. I couldn’t let the umbrella go to waste. I snatched Hector’s water bottle and we made it rain. Literally splashed them with water. Was it worth it? They froze. They got wet. But I definitely think it was worth it. Most importantly, the umbrella was used 🙂

Enjoy some of my faves from this Beacon engagement session.

We met this awesome couple in the beginning of covid. Everything was all up in the air and super confusing. We shot their engagement session at the Brooklyn Bridge and thought we’d be shooting their wedding in just a couple of months. That wasn’t the case.

It almost felt like there were hundreds of people with all the good energy in there.

Christine and Shawn met in college. Their friends and family made sure to do a little Bollywood dance explaining their love story while putting their own twist to it. These two are loved by so many! It was just so obvious just by seeing their dearest family and friends there.

Their wedding took place in Shoreham, NY. Champagne bottles were popped, confetti explosions, fog machines, life, color, love, tears, bubbles everywhere, laughs. So many emotions in one day. We hope these photos do their day justice and you get to relive this day through them.

title.rendered: Gather Greene – Coxsackie, NY, Anniversary | Tica + Justin

content.rendered:
What do you do when you’re in the middle of a pandemic and need to forget that you’re still in 2020 and it happens to be your 7th anniversary?

Easy. You go to Gather Greene.

*** BRIDES, SURE YOU READ TIL THE END ***

These lux, middle of the woods, tiny, woodsy, scandinavian, artsy, boho, cabins are all you need. AND they’re pet friendly. What else can you possibly want?

Oh yeah, peace, quiet and someone to spit stars up on the sky. Yep. It’s got that too! Gather Greene is magic. Highly recommend this for any occasion. Get away, reconnect and live again.

Booking this place was very easy and safe. They take amazing precautions to ensure everyone’s safety. They’re only booking a few cabins at a time and everything gets highly, medically, disinfected as well as aerated. The check in process is 100% contactless. No overcrowding and zero overlapping with others.

The design of this place is phenomenal, my favorite part is the pavilion. You can catch the sunset and forget everything. There’s even little deer that get so SO close. That’s how untouched Gather Greene is. Oh, you might even hear a little wolf pack out in the woods. No biggie..lol!

We had thee most phenomenal anniversary where we were able to forget the world, disconnect from our phones, social media, zoom, facetime and all the digital madness of 2020. It was truly a freeing unforgettable experience. These tiny, glamping, cabins are for sure a great way to be able to be out in the sticks, unplug, while still being able to connect with the earth. We even snuck in a little photo shoot with our furry baby, Valentino. I mean, how could we not? Shot out to our tripod for hooking us up.

OK BRIDES. If you’re still reading. GET THIS:

**GATHER GREENE IS ALSO A WEDDING VENUE**

You can book the pavilion, book the cabins and have a cozy, intimate, wedding in this magical, whimsical, boho, spot. Your guests can lodge here (or not) and the best part it’s only minutes from Athens, Catskill and Hudson. There’s plenty of little spots to go to while you’re away from your routine. It’s close enough to be far enough and the best part is it’s right in our backyards. If you need any sort of information or guidance make sure to contact Jackie, she’s my new go-to, extremely helpful and awesome!

Thank you Gather Greene for an amazing time and for the BOMB pour over! Oh, Valentino says: woof woof woof…(10 out of 10)

Si and Marcos had their closest family and friends with them on their special day and they couldn’t have chosen a better day for their fall wedding. October is literally the BEST month (I mean, not trying to be partial or anything – it’s also our anni month) but it really is!

They had an intimate ceremony at a private residence in New Milford, Ct and then we hopped over to Lovers Leap for some autumn vibes.

What made this wedding even more special was having so many of our dear friends tied in watching this over zoom. A lot of people that I know would’ve loved to be here obviously couldn’t, stinking covid. Anyway, the pressure is ON since I know everyone wants to see the pics.

**anxiously hyperventilates**

Guys, I already told you this but I will REALLY miss shooting your lovely faces, thank you so much for trusting me (and putting up with me) for your proposal, engagement and wedding. Don’t have any babies otherwise I’m coming for ya!

Shot out to: Tifah @juniperglenn_ for the awesome bouquets and boutonnieres, Storeybook Cakes for the cake, Taylor for the log suggestion (totally made the day!) and everyone else for being amazing and doing all you did to make this day so special!

title.rendered: Waterfall Engagement, Connecticut | Si + Marcos

content.rendered:
For this engagement session, we decided to be a little spontaneous and go with the flow. At first, we had this whole session planned, location was set, but then we said, hey LET’S JUST DO SOMETHING DIFFERENT. So we did!

**disclaimer, we (by we I mean me) did have a mini heart attack because the location we were originally going to was temporarily closed**

Marcos was on it and found us a new spot right away. All he told Si was: make sure to wear a flowy dress, so she did! She knew nothing else. This just goes to show how easy going this couple is. They were up for it all, even getting in the water!

After all, it is the end of summer and we had to make the best of it!

***Now to the disclaimer of the disclaimers***

Not even 10 minutes into the session and Si got a little surprise visit up her flowy dress and a little bee decided to STING her. Good thing she’s not allergic, she totally survived but was definitely traumatized after that (we ALL were).

After climbing a tree log, stepping into bushes and whatnot, me and Justin feel a little prickly-sting…p o i s o n…I V Y.

To top things off, some frisky fishies were being too fresh and didn’t like the surprise guests that showed up to their water.

All in all, Si and Marcos were amazing at being so easy going, adapting to everything and making it look flawless. Just thought I’d share some fun facts from this waterfall engagement sesh. Can’t wait for the wedding guys!

title.rendered: Sunflower Maze Proposal, Connecticut | Si + Marcos

content.rendered:
She. Said. SI!

:: pun intended ::

We can’t believe we got to witness this epic proposal at a sunflower maze! We were there the moment Si and Marcos met and here we are, 10 months later, seeing him get down on one knee while she yells out: YES!

Si had no idea he was going to pop the question right this day. I mean, after all, it was supposed to be a simple, social-distanced, girls day out, while Marcos was “away” for work. Yes, this whole proposal was based on lies LOL (jk, but yes, kinda). I mean, she had to be thrown off somehow right?

Here we are, at the sunflower maze, take the wrong turn and you’re lost. (I was totally lost btw). Marcos planned this to the T. He wrote this pretty lady a beautiful poem and made sure there were cards ready to be handed to her at different points of the maze. We handed her the first clue and she was fully clueless. By the time she got to her last card (last part of the poem) she was blindfolded. Once the blindfold was off and he was down on one knee, as soon as she would read the last piece, she’d turn around to find him there.

:: spoiler alert::

SHE SAID YES!

I’ve literally spoiled the whole story and you just need to scroll down to see it live! (or I mean, captured).

She laughed, said no, jumped and screamed YES! Needless to say, this was one extremely HAPPY girl right here.
